title: The 12 That Wasn't a Eulogy
title_candidates: Two Days at the Tour, Two Days at the Stadium
How To Win a Trophy in Spite of Yourself
theme: John Patterson wins the Trophy by ten — and yet the most-talked-about hole of his week is a 12. A study in how a tournament gets decided not on the disaster but on what comes next: Williams' early lead, Patterson's R3 takeover, Jon Baker's quiet procession to a course record, and a Wooden Spoon that changed hands one hole into Sunday.
opening_hook: Open on R1 H4, Tour Course: John Patterson signing for a 12 — the worst hole-vs-par in TEG history at that point — then pull back to reveal he'd go on to win the Trophy by ten. Frame the rest as the question 'how on earth did that happen?'
foreshadow: Patterson's 12 at R1 H4 — the kind of number that usually ends a Trophy bid before it starts
Gregg Williams leading after R1 and R2 with a personal-best gross round in the bank
Jon Baker, defending Trophy champion, going quietly about Green Jacket business while everyone watches Williams
Two prior Spoons for Mullin, the reigning holder, with Alex Baker apparently doing him a favour for three rounds

competitions.name: Trophy
competitions.how: Trailed Williams by one after R1 and R2 despite a 12 at the Tour's 4th in R1 (TEG par-worst). Took the outright lead at R3 H4 on the Stadium, lost it briefly to Williams' par at H3, retook it for good at H4 (b18), then piled up 19 points across holes 12-17 (b10) to swing the round into an 8-shot cushion. Closed with 40 in R4 to win by ten.

competitions.name: Green Jacket
competitions.how: Williams led the gross race for two rounds on the back of his career-best +12 at the Tour (b07, b11). Jon Baker overtook for good at R3 H4 (b17), then sealed it on Sunday with a new Stadium course record of 89 (cr01) — the first sub-90 in 15 TEG visits to the place — to win by 8.

competitions.name: Wooden Spoon
competitions.how: Alex Baker held the bottom position from R1 H14 through R3 H17, swapping with Mullin twice but always reclaiming it. Then R4 H3: Mullin made a 9, dropped to last (b19), and didn't climb back. Worth noting Mullin is the reigning holder coming in.

headline_candidates: A 12, a 105, and a Leader Nobody Mentions
The Tour Course Gets Its Pound of Flesh
rounds.chosen_headline: A 12, a 105, and a Leader Nobody Mentions
rounds.angle: Williams quietly posts a career-best gross 84 to lead both competitions, while Patterson takes a 12 at SI 2 and Neumann sets a new Tour course-worst of 105. The leaderboard says one thing; the conversation in the bar says another.

Williams holds the one-shot Trophy lead and the one-shot Jacket lead, but Patterson keeps closing — a 10 at the 15th the only thing keeping him from drawing level on Stableford. The Tour Course is finished; the Stadium awaits.

headline_candidates: Four Holes That Decided a TEG
rounds.angle: The decisive round. Patterson and Jon Baker both take outright leads at H4 (b18, b17); Williams responds with a 9 at the same hole (b15) and later a septuple-bogey 11 at the 13th (b05). Patterson piles up 19 points from 12-17 (b10) and goes to bed eight clear. Mullin briefly bottoms the Spoon race before Alex Baker reclaims it.

headline_candidates: Mullin's Sunday Morning, Baker's Sunday Afternoon
The Record, The Spoon, and the Eagle
rounds.chosen_headline: Mullin's Sunday Morning, Baker's Sunday Afternoon
rounds.angle: Mullin's 9 at the 3rd hands him the Spoon (b19), reversing three rounds of Alex Baker holding it. Jon Baker shoots 89 to break the Stadium course record (cr01) and seal the Jacket by 8. Stuart Neumann eagles the 15th (b03) — the only eagle of the week. Patterson signs for 40 and a ten-shot Trophy win.

players.arc: Bookends a R1 12 with a Trophy win by ten — his second Trophy, and a redemption after finishing 6th, 4th in his last two TEGs. New Tour course PB (96) into the bargain.

Led for two rounds, posted a career-best gross round (+12) and a career-best gross total (+76), and finished runner-up in both Trophy and Jacket. Two course PBs, no silverware — his third bridesmaid Trophy finish.

Defending Trophy champion who quietly switched targets: led the Jacket from R3 H4 to the finish and broke the 15-visit-old Stadium course record on Sunday with an 89. A first Green Jacket since TEG-knows-when.

Reigning Spoon holder, retains the title via a single back-nine — sorry, front-three — wobble at the Stadium on Sunday. A 9 and an 8 in the first four holes of R4 did the damage. Jacket runner-up though, and a Tour course PB.
Set a Tour course-worst 105 in R1, then ground out two course PBs and the field's only eagle at the par-5 15th in R4. Avoids the Spoon for the first time in two TEGs.
Held the Spoon position from R1 to R3 H17 — and somehow handed it back. New Stadium PB (93, down 13 on his last visit) is the saving grace.

cuts: b09 (R1 hot stretch — colour, redundant alongside b50)
b21 (Williams brief retake at R3 H3 — superseded one hole later)
b22 (Mullin hot stretch R4 — doesn't change the Spoon outcome)
b23 (Patterson hot stretch R4 — Trophy already decided)
b24, b25 (Patterson smaller blow-ups — b02, b13, b14 carry the theme)
b30, b35, b36, b37 (smaller blow-ups, not narratively decisive)
b38, b39, b40, b41 (R2 hot stretches — leaderboard barely moved)
b42, b43, b44, b45, b46, b47 (recovery stretches — colour, not spine)
b49 (Jon Baker collapse R1 — doesn't shape the Jacket arc)
b32, b33 (R3 Spoon ping-pong — b31, b34 sufficient)
venue_notes: TEG's 3rd visit to Catalonia, 4th and 5th rounds at the Stadium, 3rd and 4th at the Tour. Frame it as the now-familiar Catalunya double-header: two days of Tour (the gentler, Coles-Gallardo sibling) then two days of Stadium (Spain's premier course, host to pros, the place that had resisted a sub-90 across 15 TEG visits until Jon Baker on Sunday). Use the Tour/Stadium split as the structural pivot — Williams' lead was a Tour-Course lead; the Trophy and Jacket were both decided on the Stadium.
course_history_notes: Jon Baker's 89 at the Stadium in R4 — new course record across 15 prior visits (cr01); 1 better than his own previous best of 90 from TEG 11.
Stuart Neumann's 105 at the Tour in R1 — new course-worst across 10 prior visits (cr02).
Gregg Williams: new PBs at BOTH courses — 84 at the Tour in R1 (down 8 on TEG 6), 92 at the Stadium in R4 (down 7 on TEG 11).
John Patterson: new PBs at both courses — 96 at the Tour (down 7), 95 at the Stadium (down 4).
Alex Baker: new Stadium PB of 93 in R4 — 13 shots better than his last visit.
David Mullin: new Tour PB of 84 in R2 — narrowly (1 shot) ahead of his TEG 11 mark.
decisive_moments: Trophy: R3 H4 at the Stadium — Patterson takes the outright lead (b18) and turns a one-shot deficit into an eight-shot cushion by the end of the round.
Green Jacket: R3 H4 at the Stadium — Jon Baker takes the outright gross lead (b17) at the same hole, and never relinquishes it; cr01 in R4 puts it beyond reach.
Wooden Spoon: R4 H3 at the Stadium — Mullin's 9 at the par-5 3rd (b19) drops him below Alex Baker for the first time since R1, and stays there.

payoffs.payoff: He recovers without anyone really noticing, then wins by ten — the 12 becomes the punchline rather than the obituary.

payoffs.seed: Williams leading R1 and R2 with a career-best gross round
payoffs.payoff: Williams' 11 at the 13th (b05) and Patterson's 19-point run from 12-17 (b10) flip the eight-shot swing; Williams finishes Trophy AND Jacket runner-up, two PBs and no pots.

payoffs.seed: Jon Baker watching the Williams-Patterson duel
payoffs.payoff: Jon Baker, defending Trophy champion, sets the Stadium course record (cr01) and wins the Jacket by 8 — quietly, while everyone else was arguing about Stableford.

payoffs.seed: Mullin defending the Spoon, Alex Baker doing the work for him
payoffs.payoff: Alex Baker hauls himself off the bottom; Mullin's 9 at R4 H3 (b19) and 8 at H4 (b28) volunteer him back into the seat he never left.
